git_head: b2387d7 Probe G900 G15 cube assignment from adjacency
question: Does oriented L(Petersen) incidence or G900 provenance break the raw adjacency symmetry enough to derive the G15 cube role assignment?
answer: Orientation/provenance confirms the right search layer but does not yet derive an exact full 8/6/1 cube assignment. This run records a frontier: many label/provenance hits are present, but explicit directed-row extraction did not recover the missing assignment. Text evidence still places 6 and 9 on face-centroid roles.

oriented_petersen_control.description: The 15 vertices of L(Petersen) can be read as the 15 edges of Petersen. A bare orientation of those edges gives a 5 outer + 5 spoke + 5 inner edge-type profile, not an 8/6/1 cube profile.
oriented_petersen_control.lesson: Orientation is relevant, but bare oriented L(Petersen) edge type is not by itself the cube role assignment. The missing discriminator must be enriched provenance or boundary/package incidence.
